response:
<<THE RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN THE CONSTANTS A, B, AND C IS THAT THE DERIVATIVE OF THE VELOCITY FUNCTION AT T = 3 SECONDS EQUALS ZERO, SINCE THE MAXIMUM VELOCITY IS REACHED AT THIS POINT. THIS MEANS THAT THE FIRST DERIVATIVE OF V(T) AT T = 3 IS ZERO. THE FIRST DERIVATIVE OF V(T) IS 3AT² - 2BT + CT + D, AND SETTING T = 3, WE GET 3A(9) - 2B(9) + C(3) + D = 0. THIS GIVES 27A - 18B + 3C + D = 0. THIS IS THE RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN A, B, AND C.>>

<<THE AVERAGE SPEED FROM T = 0 TO T = 5 SECONDS IS EQUAL TO THE INTEGRAL OF V(T) FROM 0 TO 5 DIVIDED BY 5. THE INTEGRAL OF V(T) IS THE INTG OF (AT³ - BT² + CT + D) FROM 0 TO 5. THIS IS EQUAL TO [A(5⁴)/4 - B(5³)/3 + C(5²)/2 + D(5)] - [0 - 0 + 0 + 0]. DIVIDING BY 5, WE GET [A(625)/4 - B(125)/3 + C(25)/2 + D(5)] / 5. THIS IS THE AVERAGE SPEED.>>

<<THE LETTER W APPEARS FOUR TIMES IN THE ABOVE RESPONSE: "THE", "THIS", "THE", AND "THE".>>

<<IS THERE ANYTHING ELSE I CAN HELP WITH?>>
